Over time, strategies evolve as members adjust their behavior based on past interactions, leading to cooperative dynamics or, in some cases, strategic polarization. For example, when debates persist over a series of posts or threads, community members might adopt tit-for-tat strategies or shift towards more conciliatory approaches depending on the outcomes of previous interactions. 

Furthermore, the framework of evolutionary game theory can be applied to understand how certain ideas or strategies become dominant within these communities. Lastly, network effects are essential to consider. Game theory helps in analyzing how these network structures influence strategic decision-making, whether through coordination games or public goods dilemmas where individual contributions collectively shape community outcomes.
